# Driver Shift Break Constraints

> Enforce driver working-hours limits and mandatory rest breaks within routes. Each vehicle has a shift time window and breaks with their own time windows and durations.

**Category:** Workflow · **Version:** 1.0.0 · **Tags:** driver-hours · breaks · compliance · shift-scheduling

## What this does

Enforce driver working-hours limits and mandatory rest breaks within routes. Each vehicle has a shift time window and breaks with their own time windows and durations.

Specifies 5 acceptance outcomes that any implementation must satisfy, regardless of language or framework.

## Fields

- **shift_start** *(number, optional)* — Shift Start (s)
- **shift_end** *(number, optional)* — Shift End (s)
- **break_id** *(number, required)* — Break ID
- **break_time_windows** *(json, optional)* — Break Time Windows
- **break_service_duration** *(number, optional)* — Break Duration (s)
- **break_description** *(text, optional)* — Break Description
- **break_max_load** *(json, optional)* — Break Max Load

## What must be true

- **break_once_per_route:** Each break must appear exactly once in the vehicle's route; omitting a required break is a missing_break violation in plan mode.
- **break_between_stops:** Breaks are inserted between job stops; they cannot overlap with service at a job location.
- **break_window_timing:** A break's time window is satisfied when the break begins within the window; the same waiting-time logic used for jobs applies.
- **multiple_breaks_allowed:** Multiple breaks are allowed per vehicle and are scheduled independently.
- **break_max_load_rule:** If break_max_load is set the break may only start when vehicle load is at or below that threshold on all dimensions.
- **shift_window_enforced:** No stop (including breaks) may be scheduled outside the vehicle shift window in standard solving mode.
- **duplicate_break_rejected:** Duplicate break IDs for the same vehicle are rejected as input errors.

## Success & failure scenarios

**✅ Success paths**

- **Shift Window Exceeded** — when plan/ETA mode active; last route step completes after shift_end, then delay violation recorded; severity expressed as seconds beyond shift end.
- **Break Missed Plan Mode** — when plan/ETA mode active; break defined for vehicle but not included in submitted route steps, then missing_break violation recorded at route level.
- **Break Waiting** — when vehicle arrives at break position before the break window opens, then Waiting time added to route; break starts at window open time.
- **Break Scheduled** — when vehicle has at least one break defined; a feasible position exists in the route within the break's time window, then Break inserted between two stops; route step includes break with arrival, waiting_time, and service fields.

**❌ Failure paths**

- **No Feasible Break Position** — when standard solving mode; no position in route satisfies both break time window and max_load, then Optimizer may leave some jobs unassigned to create space for the break. *(error: `BREAK_NO_FEASIBLE_POSITION`)*

## Errors it can return

- `BREAK_DUPLICATE_ID` — Two breaks for the same vehicle share the same id.
- `BREAK_NO_FEASIBLE_POSITION` — No route position satisfies break time window and load constraints simultaneously.

## Connects to

- **vrp-solving** *(required)*
- **time-window-constraints** *(required)*
- **vehicle-capacity-constraints** *(optional)*
- **stop-eta-calculation** *(recommended)*
